hooks.command: cmd=$(jq -r '.tool_input.command // empty'); if printf '%s' "$cmd" | grep -qE 'git[[:space:]]+stash'; then printf '%s' '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","permissionDecision":"deny","permissionDecisionReason":"git stash is banned in this repo. WHY: multiple coding agents often work in this repo in parallel, and unexpected uncommitted changes in the working tree are likely another agent\u0027s in-flight work. Stashing (or reverting/discarding) silently hides that work and has caused lost changes in the past. WHAT TO DO: STOP and ASK THE USER what to do. Describe the conflicting files and wait for an answer. DO NOT try to work around this by copying files to /tmp, using git checkout/restore/reset to revert, deleting files, or any other clever maneuver \u2014 those are the same anti-pattern and will also destroy the other agent\u0027s work. The only acceptable next step is a message to the user. See AGENTS.md \u2192 Collaboration rules, feedback_no_stash, and feedback_no_temp_edit_other_agents."}}'; fi
